etymology.description: phonetic 奴(奴) (nú, "slave") + semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") -> take with the hand
etymology.interpretation: 奴(奴) (nú, "slave") provides the phonetic element, while 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") suggests the action involves manual effort, leading to the meaning "take" or "hold."

examples.chinese: 请把这个包拿给我(請把這個包拿給我)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ zhège bāo ná gěi wǒ.
examples.english: Please take this bag to me.

examples.chinese: 他必须拿出证明文件(他必須拿出證明文件)，才能进入大楼(才能進入大樓)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bìxū ná chū zhèngmíng wénjiàn, cái néng jìnrù dàlóu.
examples.english: He has to take out the proof documents to enter the building.

examples.chinese: 请先拿号(請先拿號)，然后在座位上等候(然後在座位上等候)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng xiān ná hào, ránhòu zài zuòwèi shàng děnghòu.
examples.english: Please take a number first, then wait at the seat.
